text: Quantum mechanics (Planck 1900, Bohr, Heisenberg, Schrödinger, 1920s) describes physical phenomena at atomic and subatomic scales. Fundamental principles: wave-particle duality, quantization (energy in discrete units), uncertainty principle (cannot simultaneously know position and momentum precisely), superposition, entanglement.
Double-slit experiment: single particles create interference patterns — wave-particle duality demonstrated.
